CREATE TABLE LEADS_TEST (
  ORG_ID      integer not null,
  LEAD_ID     integer not null,
  FREETEXT_ID integer not null,
  SUBJECT     varchar (255),
  NAME_FIRST  varchar (30),
  NAME_LAST   varchar (30),
  TITLE       varchar (255),
  primary key (ORG_ID, LEAD_ID));
INSERT INTO LEADS_TEST (ORG_ID, LEAD_ID, FREETEXT_ID, SUBJECT, NAME_FIRST, NAME_LAST, TITLE) VALUES (1, 1, 1000, 'Breaking News', 'John', 'Doe', 'Latest Update');
INSERT INTO LEADS_TEST (ORG_ID, LEAD_ID, FREETEXT_ID, SUBJECT, NAME_FIRST, NAME_LAST, TITLE) VALUES (1, 2, 1001, 'Sports Event', 'Jane', 'Smith', 'Game Highlights');
INSERT INTO LEADS_TEST (ORG_ID, LEAD_ID, FREETEXT_ID, SUBJECT, NAME_FIRST, NAME_LAST, TITLE) VALUES (1, 3, 1002, NULL, 'Alice', 'Johnson', 'Opinion Piece');
SELECT vtb, FREETEXT_ID FROM LEADS_TEST WHERE FREETEXT_ID IN (SELECT FREETEXT_ID FROM LEADS_TEST WHERE ORG_ID = 1);
WITH indexed_data AS (SELECT FREETEXT_ID FROM LEADS_TEST WHERE SUBJECT IS NOT NULL) SELECT COUNT(*) FROM indexed_data;
SELECT (SELECT COALESCE(COUNT(*), 0) FROM LEADS_TEST WHERE ORG_ID = 1) AS ORGANIZATION_COUNT;
